arts.ttl: Arteta says Arsenal’s performance was below the mark needed to win the title
arts.sum: 'I never felt we were at the level required,’ says manager West Ham’s Graham Potter: ‘A really positive day for us’ A “very, very angry” Mikel Arteta conceded that Arsenal’s performance as they slumped to defeat against West Ham had not been up to the standard required to win the Premier League and said his players must “feel the pain” before their trip to Nottingham Forest in midweek. Jarrod Bowen’s 50th Premier League goal sealed three points for Graham Potter’s side as the substitute Myles Lewis-Skelly was sent off in the second half. It leaves Arsenal still eight points behind Liverpool, with Arne Slot’s side having an opportunity to extend their lead when they face Manchester City on Sunday. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Man Utd learn Branthwaite price as Arsenal and Chelsea eye €100m deals: FT recap
arts.sum: Plus, Arsenal and Chelsea began plotting €100 million summer deals and Man Utd put in another 'unforgivable' performance. Man Utd learn Branthwaite price as Arsenal and Chelsea eye €100m deals: FT recap

arts.ttl: Arsenal, Mikel Arteta defended amid striker crisis as January transfer failures laid bare
arts.sum: Arsenal legend Ray Parlour explains to Sports Mole why the Gunners not signing a striker in the January transfer window could still work out for them.

arts.ttl: Paul Merson says the Premier League title is 'one million per cent' heading to Liverpool after Arsenal's loss to West Ham as he claims Mikel Merino played like a 'fish up a tree' in his new position
arts.sum: Liverpool will travel to Manchester City on Sunday but Merson claimed that even a 7-0 defeat for the leaders would not be enough to reignite Arsenal's hopes of winning the title.

arts.ttl: 'I'm very, very angry' - Arteta after Arsenal hopes suffer 'big blow'
arts.sum: The Premier League title race may not be done yet - but Mikel Arteta was "very, very angry" after Arsenal's loss to West Ham.

arts.ttl: Arsenal fail to close gap as West Ham inflict first home defeat
arts.sum: Arsenal missed the chance to cut Liverpool's lead at the Premier League summit as West Ham United won 1-0 to end the Gunners' 15-match unbeaten run and inflict their first home defeat of the season. However, Arsenal struggled to carve out chances against a well-drilled West Ham defence at Emirates Stadium and were punished when Jarrod Bowen nodded Aaron Wan-Bissaka's cross home just before half-time.

arts.ttl: Analysis: Familiar problems hurt Arsenal's title hopes
arts.sum: Football writer Alex Keble assesses Arsenal's 1-0 home defeat to West Ham United, which leaves them eight points behind league leaders Liverpool. Arsenal managed just two shots on target, their third-lowest tally of the season, and created just once big chance according to Opta, their joint-fewest in a Premier League game.

arts.ttl: Premier League forced to release Arsenal VAR statement after Myles Lewis-Skelly red card
arts.sum: Myles Lewis-Skelly was sent off for Arsenal during their Premier League clash against West Ham. The teenager came on at half-time for Riccardo Calafiori - but then departed Emirates Stadium early after VAR intervened to recommend an on-field review by Craig Pawson. "VAR checked the referee's call of yellow card to Lewis-Skelly for a challenge on [Mohammed] Kudus, deeming it denial of an obvious goal-scoring opportunity due to the goalkeeper's position and there being no covering defender, therefore recommended an on-field review.

arts.ttl: Arsenal player ratings vs West Ham as Merino fails and Lewis-Skelly sent off
arts.sum: As Arsenal tried to probe for a goal, Myles Lewis-Skelly was sent off for taking out Mohammed Kudus and being the last man in a nightmarish situation. David Raya – Let down by his defenders for the first goal, sadly; little he could do to prevent the goal after the cross was allowed to flash across him to Bowen.

arts.ttl: Furious Mikel Arteta accuses Arsenal of dropping standards in West Ham defeat
arts.sum: Liverpool could go 11 points clear if they beat Manchester City and Arteta did not hold back as he expressed his disappointment
